calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-26-2022 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-15-2022 Set For Case Management Conference On May-18-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-05-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Elisabeth A. Madden, Esq., LAW OFFICES OF MADDEN & LYNCH PC., (415) 851-5900, emadden@maddenlynch.com, for Defendant KATHY FIELDS, M.D.
No other appearances.

Matter is called at 9:34 a.m. Plaintiff, Azadeh Riaz, who contested the Court's tentative ruling was not present at the time of the calling of this motion. Matter is put aside to allow time for plaintiff to appear. Matter is recalled at 9:50 a.m. Plaintiff is not present.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

DEFENDANT KATHY FIELDS, M.D.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication

Defendant Kathy Fields, M.D.'s Motion for Summary Judgment to Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint is granted. Plaintiff has not filed an opposition to the motion. (Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b).) That Plaintiff is self-represented does not excuse these procedural deficiencies. "[T]he rules of civil procedure must apply equally to parties represented by counsel and those who forgo attorney representation." (Rappleyea v. Campbell, 8 Cal.4th 975, 984-85 (1994); see also ViaView, Inc. v. Retzlaff (2016) 1 Cal.App.5th 198, 208 ["While a party may choose to act as his or her own attorney, '[s]uch a party is to be treated like any other party and is entitled to the same, but no greater consideration than other litigants and attorneys."].)

Regardless, here the date for simultaneous disclosure of experts was September 13, 2021 and supplemental expert disclosure was due on or before October 4, 2021. To date Plaintiff has failed to disclose any expert witnesses and has not requested leave to submit a late expert disclosure. Plaintiff is thus barred from offering any contradictory expert evidence to refute Defendant's competent expert opinions in this case. (Perry v. Bakewell Hawthorne, LLC (2017) 2 Cal.5th 536, 541-542; Bruce Decl. Exhibit I.)) The Court will not consider any evidence Plaintiff presents long after expert witnesses had been designated and discovery closed. Under these circumstances, the Court concludes Defendant's motion for summary judgment is granted. Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)
